info.name: TransitOps API Local Smoke
info.description: Runner-safe smoke collection for the local Docker stack. It resets the deterministic seed outside the collection and then validates the current API surface with a mix of seeded read checks and runtime-generated write flows.

exec: pm.test("Live payload matches expected contract", function () {

request.description: Basic liveness probe against the real local API.

exec: pm.test("Ready payload confirms PostgreSQL connectivity", function () {

request.description: Readiness probe against the real local API and PostgreSQL connection.

exec: pm.test("Login returns a bearer token for the seeded admin", function () {

request.description: Authenticates with the deterministic seeded admin and stores the bearer token for the protected smoke flow.

exec: pm.test("User list includes the deterministic seeded users", function () {

request.description: Verifies the seeded admin-only user list against the deterministic dataset.

exec: pm.test("Seeded operator detail matches expected fields", function () {

request.description: Verifies seeded user detail for the deterministic operator account.

exec: pm.test("Operator login returns an operator token", function () {

request.description: Authenticates with the deterministic seeded operator to verify admin-only protection on user management.

item.name: Get Users As Operator Returns Forbidden

exec: pm.test("User list as operator returns 403", function () {
pm.test("Operator receives the coherent authorization error code", function () {

request.description: Confirms that operator tokens cannot access the admin-only user-management endpoints.

exec: pm.test("Runtime user is created as an active operator", function () {

request.description: Creates a runtime-managed user that can be mutated safely inside the smoke flow.

exec: pm.test("Runtime user role change returns 200", function () {
pm.test("Runtime user role change promotes the user to admin", function () {

request.description: Promotes the runtime user to admin to verify the role-change path.
Deactivates the runtime user while another admin still exists, covering the deactivate path without violating the last-active-admin rule.

exec: pm.test("Runtime user is reactivated as admin", function () {

request.description: Reactivates the runtime user so the smoke flow covers both activation directions.

exec: pm.test("Runtime user detail reflects the latest role and activation state", function () {

request.description: Reads back the runtime managed user after the role and activation changes.

exec: pm.test("Transport list exposes the deterministic seeded rows", function () {

request.description: Verifies the base seeded transport list against PostgreSQL.

exec: pm.test("Filter returns the planned seeded transport for the seeded assignment", function () {

request.description: Verifies the demo filters/pagination on deterministic seeded rows.

exec: pm.test("Seeded transport detail matches expected fields", function () {

request.description: Verifies seeded transport detail against the deterministic dataset.

exec: pm.test("Vehicle list includes the seeded active vehicles", function () {

request.description: Verifies the seeded vehicle list against PostgreSQL.

exec: pm.test("Seeded vehicle detail matches expected fields", function () {

request.description: Verifies seeded vehicle detail against the deterministic dataset.

exec: pm.test("Driver list includes the seeded active drivers", function () {

request.description: Verifies the seeded driver list against PostgreSQL.

exec: pm.test("Seeded driver detail matches expected fields", function () {

request.description: Verifies seeded driver detail against the deterministic dataset.

item.name: Get Shipment Events By Seeded Transport

exec: pm.test("Shipment event history is chronological and traceable", function () {

request.description: Verifies the seeded shipment-event history and actor traceability against the real API.

exec: pm.test("Vehicle create persists the runtime identifiers", function () {

request.description: Creates a runtime vehicle with a unique reference for the current smoke run.

exec: pm.test("Vehicle update returns the updated fields", function () {

request.description: Updates the runtime vehicle while keeping it active for the later assignment flow.

exec: pm.test("Runtime vehicle detail reflects the updated values", function () {

request.description: Reads back the runtime vehicle after the update.

item.name: Get All Vehicles Contains Runtime Vehicle

exec: pm.test("Vehicle list with runtime row returns 200", function () {
pm.test("Vehicle list contains the runtime vehicle", function () {

request.description: Confirms that the runtime vehicle appears in the active list.

exec: pm.test("Driver create persists the runtime identifiers", function () {

request.description: Creates a runtime driver with unique business identifiers for the current smoke run.

exec: pm.test("Driver update returns the updated fields", function () {

request.description: Updates the runtime driver while keeping it active for the later assignment flow.

exec: pm.test("Runtime driver detail reflects the updated values", function () {

request.description: Reads back the runtime driver after the update.

item.name: Get All Drivers Contains Runtime Driver

exec: pm.test("Driver list with runtime row returns 200", function () {
pm.test("Driver list contains the runtime driver", function () {

request.description: Confirms that the runtime driver appears in the active list.

exec: pm.test("Transport create returns the planned transport contract", function () {

request.description: Creates a runtime transport that the rest of the smoke flow will mutate.

exec: pm.test("Runtime transport detail reflects the create step", function () {

request.description: Reads back the runtime transport immediately after creation.

exec: pm.test("Transport update returns the updated fields", function () {

request.description: Updates the runtime transport before assignment.

exec: pm.test("Transport assignment links the runtime vehicle and driver", function () {

request.description: Assigns the runtime vehicle and driver together to the runtime transport.

exec: pm.test("Shipment event create persists actor traceability", function () {

request.description: Creates a runtime shipment event on the runtime transport using the currently authenticated seeded admin as actor.

exec: pm.test("Runtime shipment event history returns 200", function () {
pm.test("Runtime shipment event history includes the created event", function () {

request.description: Reads back the runtime shipment-event history after creating the event.

exec: pm.test("Filtered runtime transport list returns 200", function () {
pm.test("Filtered runtime transport list includes the assigned runtime transport", function () {

request.description: Verifies that the assigned runtime transport can be retrieved through the transport demo filters.

item.name: Transition Runtime Transport To In Transit

exec: pm.test("Transition to in_transit captures the actual pickup timestamp", function () {

request.description: Starts the runtime transport trip and verifies the lifecycle timestamp capture.

exec: pm.test("Transition to delivered captures the actual delivery timestamp", function () {

request.description: Completes the runtime transport lifecycle and verifies the delivery timestamp capture.

item.name: Get Delivered Runtime Transport By Id

exec: pm.test("Delivered runtime transport detail returns 200", function () {
pm.test("Delivered runtime transport detail reflects the terminal state", function () {

request.description: Reads back the runtime transport after the lifecycle transitions.
Soft-deletes the runtime transport so the smoke run also covers the delete endpoint.

item.name: Get Deleted Runtime Transport By Id

exec: pm.test("Deleted runtime transport detail returns 404", function () {
pm.test("Deleted runtime transport returns the not found error code", function () {

request.description: Confirms that the deleted runtime transport no longer appears in active reads.
Soft-deletes the runtime vehicle after the transport flow has been closed.

item.name: Get Deleted Runtime Vehicle By Id

exec: pm.test("Deleted runtime vehicle detail returns 404", function () {
pm.test("Deleted runtime vehicle returns the not found error code", function () {

request.description: Confirms that the deleted runtime vehicle no longer appears in active reads.
Soft-deletes the runtime driver after the transport flow has been closed.

item.name: Get Deleted Runtime Driver By Id

exec: pm.test("Deleted runtime driver detail returns 404", function () {
pm.test("Deleted runtime driver returns the not found error code", function () {

request.description: Confirms that the deleted runtime driver no longer appears in active reads.
